[[File:Dual macuahuitl detail.png|150px|left]]
The '''dual macuahuitl''' (in English pronounced roughly mah-KWA-weetl) is a [[non-degradable]] two-handed [[spiked]] weapon that requires level 70 [[Attack]] and 75 [[Strength]] to equip, which is obtained as a possible reward from the [[Lunar Chest]] in [[Neypotzli]].

The weapon [[Multi-hit weapons|hits twice per attack]], spaced 1 game tick apart when attacking monsters, and dealing damage in two [[hitsplats]], with both hitsplats having their max hit halved. If the player has an odd [[Maximum melee hit|maximum hit]], the first hit's maximum damage will be rounded down and the second hit's will be rounded up, e.g. a maximum hit of 25 will be split into two maximum hits of 12 and 13. 

Each hitsplat has its own accuracy check. However, unlike other two-hit weapons like [[Torag's hammers]] or [[sulphur blades]], where both checks are independent (either can hit or miss without affecting the other, equivalent to two separate hits with a single-hit weapon), the Dual macuahuitl checks the two hits sequentially, and the second check will only proceed if the first succeeds.

If the first accuracy check succeeds, damage will be rolled for the first hit, and a second accuracy check will then be performed for the second hit. If the first accuracy check fails, the second check will not proceed at all, and both hits will deal no damage. As a result, one "half" of the weapon's DPS functions normally, while the other "half" is reduced due to requiring both accuracy checks to succeed.

This is an intentional design{{CiteVideo|name=maracas|site=youtube|title=Fortis Colosseum & Perilous Moons Q&A Livestream!|timestamp=2253s|author=Old School RuneScape|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n9py_1vGiMQ&t=2253s|date=27 October 2023}} to push the weapon into a niche against targets with lower [[Defence]] levels and mirrors the attacks of the [[Moons of Peril]], which check accuracy in the same way.

As a slight counterbalance to this drawback, the dual macuahuitl has relatively high attack bonuses for its attack speed and level requirements. Its [[Crush]] bonus substantially exceeds that of the [[abyssal bludgeon]] and rivals the combined crush bonus of an [[inquisitor's mace]] and [[avernic defender]].

==Special attack==
[[File:Blood Infusion.gif|thumb|220px|A player performing the dual macuahuitl's special attack, ''Blood Infusion''.]]
The dual macuahuitl has a [[Special attacks|special attack]], ''Blood Infusion'', costing 25% special attack energy and requiring the full [[blood moon armour]] set to use.

Blood Infusion removes the sequential accuracy check and raises the player's minimum and maximum hit by 25% for that attack, whilst taking damage equal to 25% of their current [[Hitpoints]], rounded down (dealing no damage when less than 4 health). Additionally, the [[Blood moon armour#Set effect|''Bloodrager'']] set effect is guaranteed to trigger if the attack hits; if the special attack fails, the player will still take the damage used for it.

Within the [[Nightmare Zone]], the special attack will remove [[absorption]] charges if above 4 HP, dealing 0 damage to the player.

==Set effect==
When worn with all of the other pieces of the [[blood moon armour]] set, the player gains the ''Bloodrager'' set effect where the dual macuahuitl has a 33% chance upon a [[successful hit]] to attack one [[tick]] earlier than usual, allowing the player to attack again after 3 ticks. Both hits count towards this independently{{NamedRef|maracas}}, meaning there is a 5/9 (55.5...%) chance to trigger the effect if both hits are successful. If wearing an [[amulet of blood fury]], it can drain the blood fury's charges quite quickly, due to having 2 hits per attack and attacking faster than normal with the set effect.

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 3 April 2024
|update = Varlamore Tweaks & Drop Rates
|change = 
* The dual macuahuitl's [[Weapons/Categories|weapon category]] has been changed from blunt to spiked, giving it a stab attack style as a result.
* The ''Blood Infusion'' special attack now increases damage as originally intended.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Bugs==
* During the Clones special attack phase of the Eclipse Moon fight, only a single hitsplat is dealt, rather than the two the weapon should deal; other multi-hit weapons, such as the [[scythe of Vitur]] and the [[sulphur blades]], also suffer from this bug, resulting in only one hitsplat.

== Trivia ==
* A {{WP|macuahuitl}} is a wooden club with obsidian blades embedded in its shaft. It was prominently used amongst the Aztecs and other Mesoamerican civilizations for over 2,400 years. In the Nahuatl (Aztec) language, macuahuitl means "hand-wood" and is pronounced /maːk.kʷa.witɬ/.
** Some [[Guard (Varlamore)|Varlamorian guards]] and [[undead one]]s in [[Shilo Village (location)|Shilo Village]] carry a more basic one-handed macuahuitl that is unobtainable by players.
* In Nahuatl, the '-tl' in macuahuitl is pronounced as a {{WP|voiceless alveolar lateral affricate}}. This is achieved by first placing the tongue as if to make a 't' sound, then pushing out the air like in the 'l' sound (lowering the sides of the tongue). When done correctly, it resembles a harder 'th' or softer 'ch'.
